title: provider orchestration: make ProviderRuntimeIngestion the mandatory production output seam
description: Converge production provider output behind ProviderRuntimeIngestion or explicitly reclassify exceptions before reactor completion. Current evidence: translateProviderRuntimeEventToDomain is still called directly in event-sink.ts, relay-event-sink.ts, and claude-subagent-materializer.ts in addition to provider-runtime-ingestion-service.ts.
notes:
Implemented the ProviderRuntimeIngestion production-output seam slice.

Changes:
- ProviderTurnService now returns a fail-closed Claude event sink when ProviderRuntimeIngestion is unavailable, instead of allowing production-created Claude output to fall back to local relay-event-sink translation.
- Added `ProviderRuntimeIngestionRequired` for the missing-ingestion failure path.
- Updated ProviderTurnService tests so successful Claude provider output delegates to a fake ProviderRuntimeIngestion service.
- Added a runtime-boundary grep guard requiring ProviderTurnService to fail closed without ingestion and to pass ingestion into `createRelayEventSink`.
- Reclassified remaining direct translator call sites with source comments: `EventSinkImpl` is a legacy EventStore test/compat adapter, `relay-event-sink` local translation is for translator/compatibility tests, and Claude subagent transcript materialization is offline transcript staging rather than live provider output.

Acceptance evidence:
- Red/green behavior test: Claude provider output from ProviderTurnService fails with `ProviderRuntimeIngestionRequired` when the ingestion service is missing.
- Verification passed: `pnpm check`.
- Verification passed: combined focused suite, 15 files / 218 tests.
close_reason: Made ProviderTurnService-created Claude output fail closed without ProviderRuntimeIngestion, guarded the boundary, and reclassified remaining direct translator adapters.

title: provider orchestration: move session binding behind durable read model
description: Implement the Provider Session Binding Read Model cutover. Current evidence: src/lib/provider/orchestration-engine.ts still owns private sessionBindings and handlers/services call getProviderForSession. Acceptance should prove a fresh engine recovers provider binding from persisted events and handlers do not observe an engine-owned mutable map.
notes:
Implemented the provider session binding read-model cutover slice.

Changes:
- Added `ProviderSessionBindingReadModel` with an in-memory compatibility implementation and `SqliteProviderSessionBindingReadModel` backed by the existing `session_providers` projection.
- Removed the engine-owned `sessionBindings` map from `OrchestrationEngine`; binding reads/writes now delegate through the injected read model.
- Wired `persistenceDbPath` from `createProjectRelay` into `makeOrchestrationRuntimeLayer`, which opens a scoped SQLite read-model handle when persistence is configured.
- Preserved synchronous handler compatibility through the existing engine methods while moving the authoritative persisted lookup behind `session_providers`.

Acceptance evidence:
- Added a fresh-engine behavior test that seeds `session_providers` and dispatches `interrupt_turn` without any prior `bindSession` call.
- Added a relay-style wiring test proving `makeOrchestrationRuntimeLayer({ persistenceDbPath })` reads persisted bindings.
- Verification passed: `pnpm check`.
- Verification passed: provider-orchestration focused suite, 11 files / 85 tests.
close_reason: Moved session binding behind an injected read-model seam, added SQLite-backed session_providers lookup, wired persistenceDbPath into relay orchestration, and verified with focused tests plus pnpm check.

title: Finish durable provider orchestration cleanup
description: Extracted from the May 18 durable receipts/decider/projector plan plus architecture and TDD improvement notes. Current main has migrations, receipts, command commit, decider, read-model, queue, and side-effect reactor modules, but the plan endpoint is not reached: OrchestrationEngine still owns an in-memory sessionBindings map, handlers still query getProviderForSession from that engine, and multiple production paths call translateProviderRuntimeEventToDomain directly outside ProviderRuntimeIngestion.

title: provider orchestration: reconcile partial implementation against May 18 plans
description: Start with a focused audit of current source versus the target plan, architecture-improvements note, and TDD improvements. Mark completed slices explicitly, preserve existing behavior, and sequence only the remaining gaps before editing.
design:
Focused reconciliation against the May 18 provider-orchestration plan, architecture-improvements note, and TDD-improvements note.

Baseline verified before edits:
- `pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-engine-effect.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-contracts.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-commit.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-read-model.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-decider.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-queue.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-side-effect-reactor.test.ts test/unit/provider/provider-runtime-ingestion.test.ts`
- Result: 8 files, 34 tests passed.

Completed or partially completed on current main:
- Durable schema surfaces exist: `command_receipts`, `provider_command_outbox`, `provider_command_events`, and `provider_command_meta` are represented by the current orchestration contract tests and repository modules.
- Core modules exist: `orchestration-command-contracts.ts`, `orchestration-command-commit.ts`, `orchestration-read-model.ts`, `orchestration-decider.ts`, `orchestration-command-queue.ts`, and `orchestration-side-effect-reactor.ts`.
- Same-process command-id enforcement and in-flight duplicate handling exist in `OrchestrationEngine.dispatchEffect`.
- `ProviderRuntimeIngestion` is implemented and covered directly, and the relay event sink can delegate to it when supplied.

Remaining gaps to implement, in order:
1. `conduit-test-cev.2`: cut provider session binding over to a durable read model. `OrchestrationEngine` still owns `private readonly sessionBindings = new Map<string, string>()`, handlers/services still call `getProviderForSession`, and shutdown still clears authoritative binding state.
2. `conduit-test-cev.3`: make `ProviderRuntimeIngestion` the mandatory production output seam. Direct calls to `translateProviderRuntimeEventToDomain` still exist in `event-sink.ts`, `relay-event-sink.ts`, and `claude-subagent-materializer.ts`; exceptions need either removal or explicit reclassification before reactor completion.
3. `conduit-test-cev.4`: finish durable command identity and restart duplicate semantics. Current dispatch still performs provider side effects directly, `inFlightCommands` is process-local, and durable receipt replay/fingerprint mismatch handling is not wired into the engine path.
4. `conduit-test-cev.5`: harden reactor recovery/backoff/quiescence and add cleanup guards only after the behavior above is green.

Execution constraints:
- Preserve existing behavior while moving one boundary at a time.
- Use behavior tests before static guards.
- Do not introduce broad abstractions beyond the named read-model/ingestion/command-orchestration seams already in the plan.
- Before coding `conduit-test-cev.4`, pin the Wave 0 command contract choices in tests: duplicate return shape, crash policy, command-id source, deterministic time/id ownership, and effective fingerprint fields/examples.

title: Wave 0 durable command contract artifact
description: Create the shared command/receipt/read-model/outbox contract artifact before Wave 1 streams.
notes: Created Wave 0 durable command contract artifact at src/lib/provider/orchestration-command-contracts.ts. It pins receipt statuses, durable transaction row ownership, fingerprint field names, and shared TypeScript types for receipt writes, outbox requests, fingerprints, and commit plans. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-contracts.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-read-model.test.ts passed.

title: Phase 0 command read-model storage contract
description: Record durable command read-model rows, tombstone retention, and handoff policy.
design:
Command read model is command-owned, not a reuse of UI projection tables. The command transaction atomically writes durable command events, command_receipts, command read-model rows, command outbox rows, and command read-model cursor/meta. Existing UI/relay projections may lag and recover separately.

Durable rows:
- events remain the durable event boundary.
- command_receipts extends with command_type, project_key, fingerprint_hash, fingerprint_version, accepted_sequence, side_effect_sequence, result_sequence, error_code, updated_at; statuses are accepted, rejected, side_effect_requested, side_effect_completed, side_effect_failed.
- provider_command_sessions: project_key, session_id, provider_id, provider_kind, provider_session_id, status, active_turn_id, last_sequence, timestamps, tombstoned_at, tombstone_reason, retain_until.
- provider_command_turns: project_key, session_id, turn_id, command_id, status, message id pointers only, side_effect_sequence, result_sequence, error_code, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- provider_command_interactions: project_key, session_id, interaction_id, turn_id, kind, status, request/result sequence pointers, timestamps, tombstone fields.
- provider_command_tombstones: project_key, scope_kind, scope_id, optional session_id/turn_id, causation_command_id, event_sequence, reason_code, tombstoned_at, retain_until, minimal redacted details_json.
- provider_command_outbox: request_sequence primary key, command_id, project_key, session_id, provider_id, effect_type, payload_json, status, retry columns, result/error pointers, timestamps.
- provider_command_meta: project_key, last_applied_sequence, schema_version, rebuilt_at.

Tombstones retain indefinitely in Phase 0 with retain_until NULL. Current event/receipt eviction must not delete command tombstones or new command rows until explicit expired-command behavior is designed.

Expose a narrow orchestration-read-model bootstrap(projectKey): CommandReadModelSnapshot facade. It may query only command-owned tables, command_receipts, outbox, command meta, and targeted events sequence checks. It must not hydrate shell/UI snapshots or query messages/message_parts/read-query session lists. Startup recovers/rebuilds command read model if lagging and fails closed before provider lookup if recovery fails.

title: Phase 0 deterministic orchestration id and time seams
description: Decide injected clock/id service ownership and failure semantics before receipt consumption.
design: Use one orchestration-owned deterministic runtime seam. Time comes from Effect Clock.currentTimeMillis in provider command orchestration; tests use TestClock. IDs come from a provider-orchestration ID service with deterministic test implementation and live randomUUID implementation. Caller commandId remains durable identity; no fallback command IDs. Generate only artifact IDs that actually exist: turn/orchestration event/outbox IDs, not receipt IDs unless later storage proves it. Fresh command flow reads receipts first, validates provider/read-model readiness, then obtains nowMs and IDs, then consumes/commits. Duplicate reads must not advance clock or ID services. Missing/invalid commandId, ID failure, or clock failure fail before receipt writes, provider lookup, or side effects with typed orchestration errors. DB failure after ID allocation discards IDs because no durable boundary crossed.

title: Phase 0 effective dispatch fingerprint fields
description: Decide effective dispatch identity fields including provider instance, selected model, normalized options/defaults, runtime/interaction mode, settings-backed modes, and cwd/worktree when material.
design: Fingerprint policy uses fingerprintVersion 1 over a canonical effective provider dispatch object. Include commandType, sessionId, providerId, stable providerInstanceId, runtimeMode, interactionMode, normalized workspaceRoot, final prompt text after attachment expansion, ordered image digests, effective provider/model/API model actually sent, sorted material option pairs, and material resolved defaults with explicit sentinels. Exclude commandId from hash body, originId, clientId, projectSlug, rate-limit data, turnId, receipt ids/timestamps, event sequence ids, eventSink, AbortSignal, callbacks, provider handles, loggers, raw history, whole providerState, raw env, raw UI option objects, drafts, and full image data. Normalization lives at provider command boundary after settings/model/workspace resolution and before receipt lookup or side effects; persistence stores version plus hash and optional redacted diagnostics. OpenCode variant is excluded until actually sent; stable providerInstanceId must be carried before enforcement.

title: Phase 0 crash-window recovery policies
description: Decide crash-after-accepted and crash-after-provider-completion policies before side-effect reactor work.
design: Crash-window recovery policy is fail-closed. Durable event-log completion is authoritative; Conduit must not silently replay provider side effects after restart. Crash after accepted before provider side effect recovers as orphaned/interrupted; duplicate/reconnect returns non-terminal command status with result null and no provider call. Crash after provider completion before completion events/receipt update persists recovers as incomplete side-effect state with no resend; explicit retry/reconciliation is later work. If completion events persisted but receipt did not, bootstrap repairs receipt/read-model from committed completionEventSequence. If receipt says completed but event log has no completion event, treat as corruption/recovery-required and do not call provider. send_turn is non-retryable by default unless an explicit durable retry policy proves no provider attempt started.

title: Phase 0 stable provider command id source
description: Decide the durable provider command id source and prove send_turn/interrupt_turn carry it to orchestration before provider lookup.
design:
Decision: durable provider command identity comes from a new per-command commandId supplied by the caller for browser-origin mutating commands. originId remains browser/client identity for echo suppression, rate limiting, and UI attribution; it is not command identity. The relay's existing clientId:sequence command id is process-local and must not be used as durable provider command identity unless a future relay path persists that generated id and its receipt before provider lookup or provider side effects.

Propagation policy:
- SendMessage and CancelSession RPC payloads require commandId: NonEmptyString.
- The frontend generates commandId once per user intent before the optimistic send/stop action, and retries/reconnect reuses the same id until command ack or terminal failure.
- sendMessageToSession and cancelSessionById require commandId and pass it unchanged to ProviderTurnService.
- ProviderTurnServiceSendInput and ProviderTurnServiceInterruptInput require commandId.
- ProviderTurnService includes commandId on OrchestrationEngine.dispatchEffect({ type: "send_turn", ... }) and dispatchEffect({ type: "interrupt_turn", ... }).
- Orchestration rejects mutating provider commands without commandId before provider lookup, session binding mutation, receipt consumption, or provider side effects. Phase 0 proves this for send_turn and interrupt_turn; later mutating command families follow the same rule.
- Compatibility callers that cannot supply a client command id must either fail before provider dispatch or use a persisted relay-generated id committed before dispatch. No ephemeral fallback is allowed.
close_reason: Stable provider command id source recorded and reviewed

title: Phase 0 duplicate accepted return shape
description: Decide compatibility facade return shape for same-process duplicates, restart duplicates with prior TurnResult, and restart duplicates with only ack/read-model state.
notes: Decision recorded and reviewed: TurnResult remains provider completion only; durable command facade returns SendTurnCommandOutcome with turn_result, command_status, and terminal_error variants; restart completion requires completionEventSequence; accepted cannot be mapped to a synthetic TurnResult.

title: Phase 0 receipt state model
description: Decide the durable receipt states for command acknowledgement, rejection, side-effect request/completion/failure, using focused fake-provider and SQLite proof where useful.
notes: Decision recorded and reviewed: receipt states are accepted, rejected, side_effect_requested, side_effect_completed, side_effect_failed; accepted is non-terminal; legal path is new to rejected or accepted to side_effect_requested to completed/failed; side_effect_requested stores request/outbox pointer; side_effect_completed stores event/read-model pointers.

title: Provider orchestration durable receipts and decider-projector
description: Implement the durable provider command receipts, decider/projector/read-model, queue, side-effect reactor, and handler compatibility plan from docs/plans/2026-05-18-provider-orchestration-durable-receipts-decider-projector.md. Execute sequential Phase 0 gates first, then use disjoint parallel streams only after the plan gates are green.
design: Phase 0 hard decisions must be resolved before parallel streams or handler migration. Use TDD one behavior slice at a time; parent agent owns Beads mutations during subagent work.
notes: All child phases closed. Implemented durable provider command contract, commit/read-model/decider/queue, side-effect reactor, ingestion convergence, handler commandId/reconnect compatibility, and cleanup guards. Validation: pnpm check; pnpm lint; pnpm test:unit; pnpm test:all; git diff --check all passed.

title: Wave 4 handler ownership map
description: Record exact remaining handler write sets before any Wave 4 fanout.
notes: Wave 4 ownership map resolved in implementation: prompt/send-turn/reconnect via handlers/prompt.ts, provider-turn-service.ts, and effect-handler prompt tests; interrupt/end-session/reload via ws-rpc cancel/reload handlers and orchestration engine end_session; permission/question via handlers/permissions.ts, PermissionCard.svelte, QuestionCard.svelte, and ws-rpc sessions contracts. No remaining handler fanout was left after the integrated pass.
close_reason: Resolved; handler ownership map recorded and all handler compatibility slices implemented in this worktree.

title: Prompt command handler compatibility
description: Migrate prompt/send-turn compatibility and reconnect behavior.
notes: Completed handler and reconnect compatibility. Mutating provider commands require commandId, RPC/front-end/reload/permission/question paths forward or generate command ids, reconnect observes durable state without dispatching provider commands, and the old processedCommands cache is replaced by in-flight waiter sharing plus a static guard. Validation: engine/effect/dispatch-boundary/RPC/handler tests passed; pnpm test:unit passed; pnpm test:all passed.
close_reason: Completed.

title: Durable orchestration engine queue
description: Serialize dispatch, receipt lookup, decider, commit, and duplicate waiters.
notes: Implemented durable command queue primitive at src/lib/provider/orchestration-command-queue.ts. It serializes command work and shares same-command in-flight waiters, giving future engine integration a single boundary around receipt lookup, decider, commit, and duplicate waiters. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-queue.test.ts passed.

title: Provider side-effect reactor
description: Run provider side effects after commit, idempotently, with deterministic drain/backoff.
notes: Implemented ProviderSideEffectReactor with drain/runOnce, post-commit provider side-effect execution from provider_command_outbox, ingestion-backed event sink, completion receipts, retryable_failed state, next_attempt_at backoff, and typed unknown-effect failures. Validation: reactor tests passed; pnpm test:unit passed; pnpm test:all passed.
close_reason: Completed.

title: Provider output ingestion convergence
description: Converge provider output through ProviderRuntimeIngestion and relay publication proof.
notes: Implemented provider output ingestion convergence. ProviderRuntimeIngestion optionally publishes translated stored domain events through the relay owner; relay-stack wires the publisher to WebSocketHandler; RelayEventSink delegates provider runtime output to ProviderRuntimeIngestion when persistence exists. Validation: focused provider/relay ingestion tests passed; pnpm test:unit passed; pnpm test:all passed.
close_reason: Completed.

title: Provider command decider and projector
description: Implement decider/projector/read-model behavior against the Wave 0 contract.
notes: Implemented first decider/read-model slice against Wave 0 contract: src/lib/provider/orchestration-decider.ts plans send_turn into durable events, side_effect_requested receipt, provider_command_meta read-model write, and provider_command_outbox request without provider calls. Commit/read-model modules provide the projector/storage behavior for the planned rows. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-decider.test.ts test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-commit.test.ts passed.

title: Durable command commit
description: Implement atomic durable command event/read-model/outbox/receipt commit against the Wave 0 contract.
notes: Implemented atomic durable command commit at src/lib/provider/orchestration-command-commit.ts. Added migration 0003_durable_provider_commands.sql with extended command_receipts fields plus provider_command_* tables, wired raw and Effect migration registries, and added rollback proof that events/receipt/outbox/meta all roll back on write failure. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-command-commit.test.ts test/unit/persistence/schema.test.ts test/unit/persistence/migrations.test.ts test/unit/persistence/effect-migrations.test.ts test/unit/persistence/persistence-effect.test.ts test/unit/persistence/command-receipts.test.ts passed.

title: Command read-model snapshot bootstrap
description: Define and prove minimal tombstone-aware command read-model bootstrap without loading full UI/relay snapshot.
notes: Implemented minimal command read-model snapshot bootstrap interface at src/lib/provider/orchestration-read-model.ts. Bootstrap reads command_receipts and latest events sequence, returns receipt and tombstone-aware snapshot shape, and does not query UI/relay projection tables. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/orchestration-read-model.test.ts passed.

title: ProviderRuntimeIngestion pre-seed removal
description: Remove ProviderRuntimeIngestion session pre-seeding after event-log independence is green.
notes: Removed ProviderRuntimeIngestion session pre-seeding now that event append no longer depends on sessions. Ingestion appends mapped domain events via EventStoreEffect and projects stored events; session read-model rows are created by projection. Added provider-runtime-ingestion test proving session.created does not issue direct SQL session pre-seed. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/provider/provider-runtime-ingestion.test.ts test/unit/persistence/event-store.test.ts test/unit/persistence/schema.test.ts passed.

title: Event-log independence
description: Make durable event append independent of read-model/projection rows and prove projection creates sessions from session.created.
notes: Implemented event-log independence: removed events.session_id -> sessions.id foreign key from baseline migration, added schema/event-store tests proving session.created can append before a sessions projection row exists and durable events survive session projection deletion, and tightened projection-runner test to prove session.created creates the session projection row. Validation: pnpm vitest run test/unit/persistence/event-store.test.ts test/unit/persistence/schema.test.ts test/unit/persistence/projection-runner.test.ts passed.

title: Command identity propagation
description: Require mutating provider commands to carry commandId before lookup or side effects; start with send_turn and interrupt_turn tracer tests.
notes: Implemented command identity propagation tracer: SendMessage/CancelSession RPC require commandId; frontend send/stop creates per-action UUID command ids; RPC server, prompt handler, ProviderTurnService, and OrchestrationEngine pass commandId through for send_turn and interrupt_turn; orchestration rejects missing send_turn/interrupt_turn commandId before provider lookup/provider side effects with MissingCommandId; legacy handleMessage rejects non-empty missing-commandId messages before normal prompt side effects. Validation: focused stable-id suite passed (13 files, 151 tests), pnpm check passed earlier for this slice, biome check --write on touched files checked 23 files, and final subagent quality review approved.

title: Audit historical plans for plan-to-beads schema coverage

acceptance_criteria: One summary markdown exists per listed plan; combined summary identifies plan structures, information types, and gaps not covered by current plan-to-beads formula roles; scoped validation confirms files exist and no unresolved obvious omissions.
notes: Completed plan-to-beads schema audit. Generated 24 per-plan summaries under docs/plans/plan-to-beads-audit/summaries/ and combined summary at docs/plans/plan-to-beads-audit/combined-summary.md. Validation: summary_count=24, combined summary present, git diff --check passed for docs/plans/plan-to-beads-audit.
close_reason: Generated one subagent summary per requested plan and compiled combined plan-to-beads schema-gap summary; scoped validation passed.

acceptance_criteria: .agents/skills/plan-to-beads/SKILL.md exists; REFERENCE.md documents schema and validation; templates/ contains generic role templates with placeholders and no dry4ts/T1-specific values; git diff --check passes.
notes: Implemented plan-to-beads repo skill at .agents/skills/plan-to-beads with generic placeholder TOML templates for formula, role, and reusable contract layers. Validation: SKILL.md is 80 lines, no dry4ts/T1-specific strings in templates, git diff --check passed for the skill and Beads files.
close_reason: Created repo-local plan-to-beads skill and generic placeholder templates; scoped validation passed.
